description_long: This bungalow features a cross-gabled roof with a prominent red finish and light wood siding, giving it a warm, inviting appearance. The front facade is dominated by large, floor-to-ceiling windows and a glass door, allowing ample natural light into the main living area. Multiple rectangular windows are distributed along the sides, providing balanced illumination and ventilation throughout the structure. The building sits on a low foundation, typical of single-story residential homes, and lacks any visible porch or veranda. The roof includes skylights on one section, enhancing the modern, airy feel of the design. The overall structure is compact yet spacious, suitable for suburban or urban neighborhoods.
description: A single-story bungalow with a cross-gabled red roof, light wood siding, and large front-facing windows, featuring a modern, open design.
description_view_0: Front view showing large glass windows and door beneath the gable, highlighting the main entrance and living area.
description_view_1: Side view with three rectangular windows on the roof and wall, emphasizing the building's length and window placement.
description_view_2: Rear side view displaying additional windows and the extended section of the bungalow, with the roof's cross-gabled structure visible.
description_view_3: Back view showing the plain wooden wall with no windows or doors, and the full expanse of the gabled roof.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with large front windows and scattered side windows
